weather_line: Fog in the hollows · shelf conditions optimal · amber glow reported near the lifts

stories.headline: They've Gone: Christine and Steve Reach Heron Shelf at First Light, and What the Hollow Held Was Not on Any Map
body: The Jeep departed before sunrise on Saturday, its headlamps cutting through the fog of the Jellico hollows as Christine and Steve made for Heron Shelf — the rock formation forty miles east, two bookmarks and one studied photograph in the making. The track, which the Adventure Desk had previously described as 'probably shouldn't still exist,' did still exist, though it expressed some reluctance about the last quarter mile.

The Shelf itself, sources confirm, overhangs a hollow roughly eight feet deep and wide enough for two people and a flask of tea, its ceiling a single unbroken arc of sandstone that channels sound in such a way that a whisper carries to the far wall and comes back slightly improved. Steve found the main overhang. Christine found a second, smaller one seventeen feet north that he had not known was there. She did not say anything. She didn't need to.

The Jeep, parked at the ridge and left to take in the view, was discovered at the return to be wearing a small rubber duck on the driver's mirror. Origin unknown. The Duck Bandit, it appears, ranges further than previously estimated.

stories.headline: Briefcase Moves: Object Found on Level Two Landing, Now Glowing Gold; the Man Who Tried to Open It Reports He Has 'Forgotten Why He Wanted To'
stories.byline: By our Ministry of Magic correspondent
body: The luminous briefcase, reclassified Friday from 'not a concern' to 'under active review,' has moved. Overnight it migrated from the Level Three corridor to the Level Two landing outside the Department of Magical Accidents and Catastrophes — a journey of approximately forty feet that no corridor camera recorded and no maintenance log explains.

More pressingly: the colour has changed. Where it glowed amber on Tuesday, it now glows a steady, unhurried gold. A junior Ministry clerk named Whitmore attempted to open the clasps at half past eight this morning and found himself standing quietly with his hands at his sides, staring at the middle distance, and unable to recall what he'd intended to do. He described the experience as 'not unpleasant, just — complete.' He has since requested the rest of the day off.

The Ministry has upgraded its statement from 'under active review' to 'a matter receiving the full attention of the appropriate personnel,' while declining to confirm which personnel, what they are attending to, or whether anyone has yet asked the briefcase directly.

stories.headline: House Reports Exceptional Quiet; Three Familiars Achieve Unprecedented Saturday Accord; Biscuit Situation Described as 'Stable, for Now'
body: With Christine and Steve on the Shelf, the household settled into Saturday with what sources inside the building describe as 'a rare and suspicious peace.' All three familiars were last confirmed in the main sitting room at nine-seventeen, occupying separate warm patches of floor in a configuration the Bureau has classified a Détente of Convenience.

The biscuit tin, audited at half past ten, remains at its reported count. Lucy was observed at a distance of three feet from the tin for approximately four minutes before relocating to the window with an expression of elaborate innocence. No biscuits are confirmed missing. The Bureau is watching.

stories.headline: Duck Discovered at Heron Shelf; Bandit's Known Range Expands by Forty Miles; Investigation Formally Upgraded to 'Regional Incident'
stories.byline: By our Special Correspondent, Rubber Duck Division
body: The small yellow duck found on the Jeep's driver's mirror at Heron Shelf this morning represents the Duck Bandit's most remote confirmed placement to date: forty miles east of Jellico, accessible only by a track that required both nerve and a capable set of tyres. How the duck arrived before the Jeep is a question the Adventure Desk has been staring at for some time.

Investigators note three possibilities: the duck was placed on an outbound trail pass, teleported by an unknown enchantment, or the Bandit was already there — waiting. The third possibility is not being discussed aloud, but everyone is thinking it. The Jeep has been briefed and is taking it well.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Conducted a thorough, unhurried investigation of every room in the house between ten and eleven this morning, pausing at the biscuit tin for what she described (to no one, as she does not speak) as 'entirely routine route-planning.' Nothing was missing when the Bureau checked. Lucy found this outcome acceptable.

Ellie: Spent forty minutes this afternoon engaged in what appeared to be a meaningful staring contest with the reflection in the back of a silver spoon. She lost, blinked, and then wagged. The spoon did not wag back but she seemed to feel it wanted to.

Jersey: Having selected his lap at the end of Friday's session and committed to the arrangement in full, he was prepared to be deeply disappointed by Saturday's early departure — but discovered that the warm patch in the sitting room left by a recently vacated blanket is, in some important respects, equivalent. He has lodged no formal complaint.
founding_editor_note: That overhang at Heron Shelf — if the sandstone's running the way I think it is, you want to check the back wall for a hairline. Beautiful piece of rock, but rock doesn't hold itself up by sentiment. I'd have brought a level. — D.

in_brief.sport: Weekend Weight Report: the Jeep lifted a full load of trail gear to elevation without complaint, which is more than can be said for some people the Adventure Desk knows.
in_brief.forecast: Clear at altitude, fog below, and something gold and patient waiting on Level Two — dress accordingly.

storyline_updates.title: The Second Bookmark
storyline_updates.summary: Arc resolved: Christine and Steve reached Heron Shelf at first light on Saturday. The hollow was everything the photograph suggested and somewhat more. Christine found a second overhang Steve hadn't known about. On their return, the Jeep was wearing a rubber duck of unknown provenance — which has opened a new thread for the Duck Bandit file. The Second Bookmark is closed: they went, they saw, she found the better bit.

storyline_updates.title: The Glowing Briefcase
storyline_updates.summary: The briefcase has migrated overnight from Level Three to Level Two without explanation, and its glow has shifted from amber to gold. A clerk named Whitmore attempted to open it and found himself peacefully emptied of intention — no distress, just a complete inability to recall what he wanted. The Ministry has upgraded its statement while still naming nothing and nobody. Day 3 should reveal something about the briefcase's origin or contents — perhaps a label, a sound, or someone who recognises it from somewhere they can't quite place.

storyline_updates.title: The Duck Bandit
storyline_updates.summary: A new arc formalised from ongoing texture: the Duck Bandit placed a rubber duck on the Jeep at Heron Shelf, forty miles east of Jellico, accessible only by a difficult track. This represents the Bandit's most remote confirmed placement. The investigation has been upgraded to a Regional Incident. Day 2 should explore how the duck got there — a witness, a second vehicle, a trail sighting, or a clue on the duck itself.
